title: Goda Devi Ashtottara Sata Nama Stotram

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यानम्
शतमखमणि नीला चारुकल्हारहस्ता
स्तनभरनमिताङ्गी सान्द्रवात्सल्यसिन्धुः ।
अलकविनिहिताभिः स्रग्भिराकृष्टनाथा
विलसतु हृदि गोदा विष्णुचित्तात्मजा नः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyānam
śatamakhamaṇi nīlā chārukalhārahastā
stanabharanamitāṅgī sāndravātsalyasindhuḥ ।
alakavinihitābhiḥ sragbhirākṛṣṭanāthā
vilasatu hṛdi gōdā viṣṇuchittātmajā naḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Meditation (Dhyānam): May Gōdā — daughter of Viṣṇuchitta — shine radiant in our hearts: she whose dark complexion rivals the sapphire gem among Indras (śatamakha-maṇi, Indra's jewel); who holds a beautiful cluster of blue water-lilies (kalhāra) in her hands; whose body gently bows under the fullness of her bosom; who is an ocean of overflowing maternal love (sāndra-vātsalya); and who draws her Lord close with the flower-garlands woven into her tresses.
contentBlocks.meanings: śatamakha-maṇi = gem of Śatamakha (Indra), i.e., sapphire | nīlā = blue-dark complexioned | kalhāra = blue water-lily (Nymphaea stellata) | stana-bhara = weight of bosom | namitāṅgī = body gently bowed | sāndra-vātsalya = intense maternal affection | sindhu = ocean | alaka = tresses | sragbhiḥ = with garlands | ākṛṣṭa-nāthā = having drawn her Lord to herself

contentBlocks.original:
अथ स्तोत्रम् ।
श्रीरङ्गनायकी गोदा विष्णुचित्तात्मजा सती ।
गोपीवेषधरा देवी भूसुता भोगशालिनी ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha stōtram ।
śrīraṅganāyakī gōdā viṣṇuchittātmajā satī ।
gōpīvēṣadharā dēvī bhūsutā bhōgaśālinī ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Beginning of the Stotram — verse 1:] Śrīraṅganāyakī — the Queen of Śrīraṅgam; Gōdā — the gift of the earth; Viṣṇuchittātmajā — daughter of Viṣṇuchitta (Periyāḷvār); Satī — the true and chaste; Gōpīvēṣadharā — wearer of the Gopī's garb; Devī — the divine Goddess; Bhūsutā — daughter of Mother Earth; Bhōgaśālinī — endowed with divine splendour and enjoyment.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-raṅga-nāyakī = queen/mistress of Śrīraṅgam | gōdā = gift of/to the earth (name of Āṇḍāḷ) | viṣṇu-chitta-ātmajā = daughter of Viṣṇuchitta | satī = chaste, true | gōpī-vēṣa-dharā = one who wears the dress of a Gopī | devī = goddess | bhū-sutā = earth's daughter | bhōga-śālinī = endowed with divine enjoyment/splendour

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 2:] Tulasīkānanōdbhūtā — she who arose from a tulasī grove; Śrīdhanvipuravāsinī — resident of Śrīvilliputtur; Bhaṭṭanāthapriyakarī — she who brings joy to Bhaṭṭanātha (Periyāḷvār); Śrīkṛṣṇahitabhōginī — she who enjoys whatever pleases Śrī Kṛṣṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: tulasī-kānana = tulasī grove | udbhūtā = arisen from, born in | śrī-dhanvi-pura = Śrīvilliputtur (ancient name; dhanvin = the bow-wielder, i.e., Viṣṇu's city) | bhaṭṭanātha = title of Periyāḷvār ("lord of the learned") | priya-karī = one who does what is dear | śrī-kṛṣṇa-hita = what is beneficial/pleasing to Śrī Kṛṣṇa | bhōginī = enjoyer

contentBlocks.original:
आमुक्तमाल्यदा बाला रङ्गनाथप्रिया परा ।
विश्वम्भरा कलालापा यतिराजसहोदरी ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āmuktamālyadā bālā raṅganāthapriyā parā ।
viśvambharā kalālāpā yatirājasahōdarī ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 3:] Āmuktamālyadā — she who gave the garland she herself had worn; Bālā — the eternal maiden; Raṅganāthapriyā — beloved of Raṅganātha; Parā — the Supreme; Viśvambharā — sustainer of the universe; Kalālāpā — speaker of graceful arts; Yatirājasahōdarī — sister of Yatirāja (Rāmānuja).
contentBlocks.meanings: āmukta-mālyada = giver of the worn garland (āmukta = that which one has worn; mālya = garland; da = giver) | bālā = young girl, eternal maiden | raṅganātha-priyā = beloved of Raṅganātha | parā = Supreme, transcendent | viśvam-bharā = bearer/sustainer of the universe | kalā-lāpā = one whose speech is full of artistic grace (lāpa = speech; kalā = arts) | yatirāja = king of ascetics (title of Rāmānuja) | sahōdarī = sister

contentBlocks.original:
कृष्णानुरक्ता सुभगा सुलभश्रीः सुलक्षणा ।
लक्ष्मीप्रियसखी श्यामा दयाञ्चितदृगञ्चला ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛṣṇānuraktā subhagā sulabhaśrīḥ sulakṣaṇā ।
lakṣmīpriyasakhī śyāmā dayāñchitadṛgañchalā ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 4:] Kṛṣṇānuraktā — deeply devoted to Kṛṣṇa; Subhagā — the auspicious and fortunate; Sulabhaśrī — she in whom Śrī is easily attained; Sulakṣaṇā — bearer of all auspicious marks; Lakṣmīpriyasakhī — dear friend of Lakṣmī; Śyāmā — the dark beauty; Dayāñchitadṛgañchalā — whose glance-corners overflow with compassion.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṛṣṇa-anuraktā = deeply attached/devoted to Kṛṣṇa (anurakta = enamoured) | subhagā = endowed with su-bhāgya (good fortune) | su-labha-śrī = she in whom Śrī (Lakṣmī's grace) is easily gained | su-lakṣaṇā = bearing auspicious lakṣaṇas (marks) | lakṣmī-priya-sakhī = dear friend of Lakṣmī | śyāmā = dark-complexioned | dayā = compassion | añchita = filled with | dṛk-añchala = corner/edge of the gaze

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 5:] Phalgunyāvirbhavā — she who appeared under the Phalgunī star; Ramyā — the lovely, delightful one; Dhanurmāsakṛtavratā — she who observed the sacred vow throughout Dhanurmāsa; Champakāśōkapunnāgamālatīvilasatkachā — she whose tresses are adorned with champaka, aśōka, punnāga, and mālatī blossoms.
contentBlocks.meanings: phālgunī = Uttarāphalgunī nakṣatra (asterism) | āvirbhavā = she who appeared/manifested | ramyā = beautiful, delightful | dhanurmāsa = the Tamil month Mārgazhi (December–January) when the Dhanus/Sagittarius sun rises | kṛtavratā = one who observed the vow | champaka = Magnolia champaca | aśōka = Saraca asoca | punnāga = Calophyllum inophyllum | mālatī = Jasminum grandiflorum | vilasat = shining, playing | kacha = tresses

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 6:] Ākāratrayasampannā — endowed with the three glorious forms; Nārāyaṇapadāśritā — she who has taken refuge at Nārāyaṇa's feet; Śrīmadaṣṭākṣarīmantrarājasthitamanōrathā — she whose heart's longing rests in the glorious eight-syllabled royal mantra (Oṃ Namō Nārāyaṇāya).
contentBlocks.meanings: ākāra-traya = three forms (rūpa, kānti, aiśvarya — beauty, grace, sovereignty; or the three bodies) | sampannā = endowed with, possessing | nārāyaṇa-pada = at Nārāyaṇa's feet | āśritā = one who has taken refuge | śrīmat-aṣṭākṣarī = the glorious eight-syllabled (Oṃ Namō Nārāyaṇāya) | mantra-rāja = king of mantras | sthita = resting in, abiding in | manō-ratha = heart's desire/chariot

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 7:] Mōkṣapradānanipuṇā — expert in bestowing liberation; Manuratnādhidēvatā — the presiding deity of the jewel-mantra; Brahmaṇyā — devoted to the Vedic path and Brahman; Lōkajananī — mother of all worlds; Līlāmānuṣarūpiṇī — she who assumed human form as a divine sport.
contentBlocks.meanings: mōkṣa-pradāna = the giving of liberation | nipuṇā = expert, skilled | manu-ratna = jewel among mantras | adhidēvatā = presiding deity | brahmaṇyā = devoted to Brahman and Vedic dharma | lōka-jananī = mother of all worlds | līlā = divine sport/play | mānuṣa-rūpiṇī = she who has taken on human form

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 8:] Brahmajñānapradā — bestower of Brahman-knowledge; Māyā — the divine creative power; Sachchidānandavigrahā — she whose form is pure Being–Consciousness–Bliss; Mahāpativratā — the supreme devoted wife; Viṣṇuguṇakīrtanalōlupā — joyfully absorbed in singing the glories of Viṣṇu.
contentBlocks.meanings: brahma-jñāna = knowledge of Brahman | pradā = giver | māyā = divine creative energy | sat = pure being | chit = consciousness | ānanda = bliss | vigraha = form, embodiment | mahā-pati-vratā = great devoted wife | viṣṇu-guṇa = the qualities/glories of Viṣṇu | kīrtana = singing/chanting | lōlupā = absorbed in, eager for

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 9:] Prapannārtiharā — remover of suffering for those who surrender; Nityā — the eternal one; Vēdasaudhavihāriṇī — she who dwells in the mansion of the Vedas; Śrīraṅganāthamāṇikyamañjarī — the jewel-cluster of Śrīraṅganātha; Mañjubhāṣiṇī — the sweet-spoken one.
contentBlocks.meanings: prapanna = one who has surrendered (śaraṇāgati) | ārti = suffering, affliction | harā = remover | nityā = eternal | vēda-saudha = the palace of the Vedas | vihāriṇī = one who roams/dwells in | māṇikya = ruby | mañjarī = cluster of blossoms, bouquet | mañju = sweet, charming | bhāṣiṇī = speaker

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मप्रिया पद्महस्ता वेदान्तद्वयबोधिनी ।
सुप्रसन्ना भगवती श्रीजनार्दनदीपिका ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmapriyā padmahastā vēdāntadvayabōdhinī ।
suprasannā bhagavatī śrījanārdanadīpikā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 10:] Padmapriyā — she who loves the lotus; Padmahastā — she who holds the lotus in her hand; Vēdāntadvayabōdhinī — she who illumines the dual Vedānta (Viśiṣṭādvaita); Suprasannā — the supremely gracious one; Bhagavatī — the divine Goddess; Śrījanārdanadīpikā — the radiant lamp of Śrī Janārdana.
contentBlocks.meanings: padma-priyā = she who loves the lotus | padma-hastā = lotus-handed | vēdānta = the end/culmination of the Vedas (Upaniṣads) | dvaya = dual (referring to Dvaya rahasya or Viśiṣṭādvaita) | bōdhinī = illuminer, teacher | su-prasannā = most gracious, most pleased | bhagavatī = the Goddess | janārdana = Viṣṇu (he who agitates/is prayed to by all) | dīpikā = lamp, light

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 11:] Sugandhāvayavā — she whose limbs are naturally fragrant; Chāruraṅgamaṅgaladīpikā — the auspicious lamp of beautiful Raṅgam; Dhvajavajrāṅkuśābjāṅkamṛdupādalatāñchitā — she whose tender foot-creepers are marked with the four auspicious signs: the flag, thunderbolt, goad, and lotus.
contentBlocks.meanings: sugandha = fragrance | avayava = limb | chāru = beautiful | raṅga = Śrīraṅgam | maṅgala = auspicious | dīpikā = lamp | dhvaja = flag | vajra = thunderbolt | aṅkuśa = elephant goad | abja = lotus | aṅka = mark | mṛdu = tender, soft | pāda-latā = foot-creeper (poetic: slender feet) | añchitā = adorned with

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 12:] Tārakākāranakharā — whose toenails are shaped like shining stars; Pravālamṛdulāṅguḻī — whose toes are as tender and rosy as coral; Kūrmōpamēyapādōrdhvabhāgā — whose upper foot arches like a tortoise's back; Śōbhanapārṣṇikā — whose heels are beautiful.
contentBlocks.meanings: tārakā = star | ākāra = shaped like | nakharā = nails | pravāla = coral (Corallium rubrum) | mṛdula = tender, soft | aṅguḻī = toe/finger | kūrma = tortoise | upamēya = comparable to | pāda-ūrdhva-bhāga = upper/dorsal part of the foot | śōbhana = beautiful | pārṣṇikā = relating to the heel

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 13:] Vēdārthabhāvatattvajñā — she who knows the inner essence and truth of the Vedas; Lōkārādhyāṅghripaṅkajā — whose lotus feet are worshipped by all worlds; Ānandabudbudākārasugulphā — whose graceful ankles are like bubbles of bliss; Paramāṇukā — of most delicate and subtle form.
contentBlocks.meanings: vēda-artha = meaning of the Vedas | bhāva = inner feeling, living essence | tattva = truth, reality | jñā = knower | lōka = world | ārādhya = worthy of worship | aṅghri-paṅkaja = lotus feet | ānanda = bliss | budbuda = bubble | ākāra = shape | su-gulpha = beautiful ankle | paramāṇu = atom (the subtlest particle) | kā = one who is

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 14:] Tējaḥśriyōjjvaladhṛtapādāṅguḻisubhūṣitā — beautifully adorned with lustrous toe-rings ablaze with radiant splendour; Mīnakētanatūṇīrachārujaṅghāvirājitā — whose graceful calves shine like the quiver of Kāmadēva, the fish-bannered god of love.
contentBlocks.meanings: tējas = radiance, lustre | śrī = divine splendour | ujjvala = blazing | dhṛta = borne, worn | pāda-aṅguḻi = toe | su-bhūṣita = beautifully adorned | mīna-kētana = fish-bannered (epithet of Kāmadēva) | tūṇīra = quiver | chāru = beautiful | jaṅghā = calf | virājitā = shining, resplendent

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 15:] Kakudvajjānuyugmāḍhyā — whose paired knees are beautifully rounded like a bull's hump; Svarṇarambhābhasakthikā — whose thighs are like golden plantain trunks; Viśālajaghanā — of wide, beautiful hips; Pīnasuśrōṇī — of full, gracefully formed buttocks; Maṇimēkhalā — wearer of a jewelled girdle.
contentBlocks.meanings: kakud = the hump of a bull (Bos indicus) | vaj = like, resembling | jānu-yugma = pair of knees | āḍhyā = endowed with | svarṇa = gold | rambhā = plantain/banana plant | ābha = resembling | sakthikā = thighs | viśāla = wide, broad | jaghanā = hips | pīna = full, rounded | su-śrōṇī = beautiful buttocks | maṇi = gem | mēkhalā = girdle, belt

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 16:] Ānandasāgarāvartagambhīrāmbhōjanābhikā — whose deep navel is like a lotus-whirlpool in the ocean of bliss; Bhāsvadvalitrikā — who bears three luminous folds on her abdomen; Chārujagatpūrṇamahōdaryai — whose beautiful great belly contains the entire universe.
contentBlocks.meanings: ānanda-sāgara = ocean of bliss | āvarta = whirlpool | gambhīra = deep | ambhoja = lotus | nābhi = navel | bhāsvat = radiant, luminous | vali-trikā = three folds (trivali, a classical sign of beauty) | chāru = beautiful | jagat-pūrṇa = full of the universe | mahā-udarī = one of great belly

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 17:] Navavallīrōmarājī — whose fine line of abdominal hair is like a tender new creeper; Sudhākumbhāyitastanī — whose breasts are like pots brimming with nectar; Kalpamālānibhabhujā — whose arms resemble the divine wish-fulfilling garland; Chandrakhaṇḍanakhāñchitā — whose nails are as lovely as crescent moons.
contentBlocks.meanings: nava-vallī = freshly sprouted creeper | roma-rājī = line of body hair | sudhā = nectar, amṛta | kumbha = pot, pitcher | āyita = resembling | stanī = one with breasts | kalpa-mālā = wish-fulfilling garland (from the kalpa tree) | nibha = resembling | bhujā = arm | chandra-khaṇḍa = crescent moon | nakha = nail | añchitā = adorned with

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 18:] Supravāśāṅguḻīnyastamahāratnāṅgulīyakā — who wears great jewelled rings upon her well-shaped, beautiful fingers; Navāruṇapravālābhapāṇidēśasamañchitā — whose palm glows with the rosy colour of freshly-blossomed red coral illuminated by the new dawn.
contentBlocks.meanings: su-pravāśa = well-shaped, beautiful (well-articulated) | aṅguḻī = finger | nyasta = placed upon | mahā-ratna = great gem | aṅguḻīyaka = ring | nava-aruṇa = newly risen (dawn-red) | pravāla = coral | ābha = glow, colour | pāṇi-dēśa = the region of the palm | samañchitā = beautifully adorned

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 19:] Kambukaṇṭhī — of conch-shaped neck; Suchubukā — of beautiful chin; Bimbōṣṭhī — of lips red as the bimba fruit; Kundadantayuk — whose teeth are even and white as jasmine buds; Kāruṇyarasaniṣyandanētradvayasuśōbhitā — whose pair of eyes shines with compassion flowing like nectar.
contentBlocks.meanings: kambu = conch shell | kaṇṭhī = one with that neck | su-chubuka = beautiful chin | bimba = Coccinia grandis (a red berry) | ōṣṭhī = one with those lips | kunda = Jasminum multiflorum (small white flowers) | danta = tooth | yuk = possessing | kāruṇya = compassion | rasa = nectar, juice, essence | niṣyanda = flowing, dripping | nētra-dvaya = pair of eyes | su-śōbhita = beautifully adorned

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 20:] Muktāśuchismitā — whose smile is pure and bright as pearls; Chāruchāmpēyanibhanāsikā — whose beautiful nose resembles the champaka blossom; Darpaṇākāravipulakapōladvitayāñchitā — adorned with two broad cheeks, smooth and bright as mirrors.
contentBlocks.meanings: muktā = pearl | śuchi = pure, clean | smitā = smiling | chāru = beautiful | chāmpēya = of/relating to the champaka flower | nibha = resembling | nāsikā = nose | darpaṇa = mirror | ākāra = form, appearance | vipula = broad, wide | kapōla = cheek | dvitaya = pair | añchitā = adorned

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 21:] Anantārkaprakāśōdyanmaṇitāṭaṅkaśōbhitā — adorned with earrings blazing with the infinite radiance of innumerable suns; Kōṭisūryāgnisaṅkāśanānābhūṣaṇabhūṣitā — wearing many ornaments ablaze like ten million suns and fires.
contentBlocks.meanings: ananta = infinite, countless | arka = sun | prakāśa = radiance | udyan = rising | maṇi = gem | tāṭaṅka = earring | śōbhita = adorned | kōṭi = ten million, crore | sūrya = sun | agni = fire | saṅkāśa = resembling | nānā = various, many | bhūṣaṇa = ornament | bhūṣita = adorned

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 22:] Sugandhavadanā — of fragrant face; Subhrū — of beautiful brows; Ardhachandralalāṭikā — whose forehead bears a crescent-moon mark; Pūrṇachandrānanā — whose face is as radiant as the full moon; Nīlakuṭilālakaśōbhitā — adorned with wavy, dark, curling tresses.
contentBlocks.meanings: su-gandha = fragrance | vadana = face | su-bhrū = beautiful brow | ardha-chandra = half-moon, crescent | lalāṭikā = relating to the forehead | pūrṇa-chandra = full moon | ānana = face | nīla = blue-black, dark | kuṭila = wavy, curling | alaka = tress, lock of hair | śōbhita = adorned

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 23:] Saundaryasīmā — the very pinnacle and summit of beauty; Vilasatkastūrītilakōjjvalā — radiant with a gleaming, sparkling musk-mark on her brow; Dhagaddhagāyamānōdyanmaṇisīmantabhūṣaṇā — whose hair-parting ornament blazes with rising, flaming jewels.
contentBlocks.meanings: saundarya = beauty | sīmā = limit, boundary, summit | vilasat = sparkling, playing | kastūrī = musk | tilaka = forehead mark | ujjvalā = radiant | dhagaddhagāyamāna = blazing (onomatopoeic: "dhag dhag" = crackling blaze) | udyan = rising | maṇi = gem | sīmanta = hair-parting | bhūṣaṇa = ornament

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 24:] Jājvalyamānasadratnadivyachūḍāvataṃsakā — who wears a divine crown-ornament of ever-blazing true gems; Sūryārdhachandravilasat bhūṣaṇāñchitavēṇikā — whose braided hair is adorned with ornaments luminous as the sun and crescent moon together.
contentBlocks.meanings: jājvalyamāna = perpetually blazing (intensive form of jval, to blaze) | sat-ratna = true/excellent gems | divya = divine | chūḍā = crown, top of head | avataṃsaka = ornament worn on the head | sūrya = sun | ardha-chandra = crescent moon | vilasat = luminous, playing | bhūṣaṇa = ornament | añchita = adorned | vēṇikā = braid, plait of hair

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 25:] Atyarkānalatējōdhimaṇikañchukadhāriṇī — who wears a bodice adorned with gems of extraordinary solar and fiery brilliance; Sadratnāñchitavidyōtavidyutkuñjābhaśāṭikā — wearing a silk garment adorned with true gems, sparkling like a bower filled with lightning.
contentBlocks.meanings: ati-arka = exceeding the sun | anala = fire | tējas = brilliance | adhi = above, exceeding | maṇi = gem | kañchuka = bodice, blouse | dhāriṇī = one who wears | sat-ratna = true/genuine gems | añchita = adorned | vidyōta = brilliance, shining | vidyut = lightning | kuñja = bower, grove | ābha = resembling | śāṭikā = silk sari/garment

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 26:] Nānāmaṇigaṇākīrṇahēmāṅgadasubhūṣitā — beautifully adorned with golden armlets studded with clusters of many and varied gems; Kuṅkumāgarukastūrīdivyachandanacharchitā — anointed with divine sandalwood paste blended with saffron, agaru, and musk.
contentBlocks.meanings: nānā = various | maṇi-gaṇa = clusters of gems | ākīrṇa = studded with, scattered with | hēma = gold | aṅgada = armlet worn on the upper arm | su-bhūṣitā = beautifully adorned | kuṅkuma = saffron/red powder | agaru = agarwood (Aquilaria malaccensis) | kastūrī = musk | divya-chandana = divine sandalwood | charchitā = anointed, rubbed

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 27:] Svōchitaujjvalyavividhavichitramaṇihāriṇī — wearing various and wonderful gem-necklaces whose lustre befits her own divine radiance; Asaṅkhyēyasukhasparśasarvātiśayabhūṣaṇā — adorned with countless ornaments of incomparably pleasant touch that surpass all other beauty.
contentBlocks.meanings: sva-uchita = befitting oneself | ōjjvalya = lustre, brilliance | vividha = various | vichitra = wonderful | maṇi = gem | hāriṇī = one who wears a hāra (necklace) | asaṅkhyēya = innumerable, beyond count | sukha-sparśa = pleasant to touch | sarva-atiśaya = surpassing everything | bhūṣaṇā = adorned with ornaments

contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 28 — final stotram verse:] Mallikāpārijātādidivyapuṣpasragañchitā — adorned with divine garlands of mallikā, pārijāta, and other heavenly flowers; Śrīraṅganilayā — she who abides in Śrīraṅgam; Pūjyā — the supremely worshipful one; Divyadēśasuśōbhitā — she who graces and beautifies all the holy divine shrines.
contentBlocks.meanings: mallikā = Arabian jasmine (Jasminum sambac) | pārijāta = night-jasmine/coral tree (Nyctanthes arbor-tristis; also the divine tree of heaven) | ādi = and others | divya-puṣpa = divine flowers | sraj = garland | añchitā = adorned | śrī-raṅga = Śrīraṅgam | nilayā = one who abides in, dweller | pūjyā = worthy of worship | divya-dēśa = divine shrines (the 108 temples of the Āḷvārs) | su-śōbhitā = beautifully gracing

contentBlocks.translation: Thus concludes the Śrī Gōdā Aṣṭōttaraśata Nāma Stotram — the hymn of the 108 names of Śrī Gōdā Devī.
